summary: Tested PublicOS P0/P1/P2 features end-to-end. Backend: 22/22 pytest tests PASS (1 skipped — positive reset-token path needs DB access) covering uploads (PNG accept, non-image reject, >10MB reject, auth required, file serve, 404), closure workflow (owner-only request, non-admin decision rejection, reject reopens to previous_status, approve→closed, timeline growth), official role (jurisdiction-scoped issues list, /official/me stats incl. Karnataka jurisdiction, status update with closed rejected, outside-jurisdiction 403, admin promote/revoke), reset-password token validate (400 for invalid), AI classify (gpt-4o-mini correctly returned 'streetlight' for obvious case, auth-required), analytics returns closure_requested + closed counts. Frontend: photo upload via Submit wizard shows preview at /api/files/{id} URL, AI re-classify button present and triggers backend call, ResetPassword pre-validates token (data-testid='reset-invalid' renders for both bogus token AND missing token — fixing prior iteration's gap), Admin /admin shows Closure requests + Officials tabs with content, Official /official portal restricted to Karnataka with stats header (0 closure pending / 4 open / 2 resolved) and 6 queue items all in Karnataka, take-action panel with status select.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1056 lines — should be split into routers (auth.py, issues.py, admin.py, official.py, storage.py) for maintainability.

CORS still uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True; cookies effectively ignored by browsers in this combo. Frontend's bearer-token fallback compensates, but tighten origins in production.

Upload endpoint trusts file.content_type from the client (could be spoofed). Consider sniffing magic bytes server-side (e.g., python-magic / Pillow.open) for defense-in-depth, especially since files are public via /api/files/{id}.

GET /api/files/{id} is public (anyone with the file_id sees the image). If sensitive proof_photos for closure should be private until approved, add an authz gate. Currently acceptable per problem statement (issues are public).

init_storage() and put_object()/get_object() do synchronous requests inside async path via asyncio.to_thread — OK for now but pool-size on requests.Session could help. Consider replacing with httpx.AsyncClient.

closure_decision endpoint stores closure.* via dotted $set — works in Mongo but couples on the closure subdoc existing; on edge cases where issue had no prior closure (already protected by status check) the keys would still be created. Safe but worth a comment.
AI classify silently returns category=None when LLM disabled — frontend should handle this gracefully (currently does, but worth a UI test for the disabled-key path).
Official panel uses custom Select component (not native <select>) so users cannot accidentally pick 'closed' from the dropdown; backend also enforces 400 if attempted — defense in depth confirmed.

success_rate.backend: 100% (22/22 v2 pytest tests pass, 1 intentional skip)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical flows: upload preview, AI re-classify visible, reset-password invalid token UI, admin Closure tab, admin Officials tab, official portal jurisdiction scoping + stats + queue

test_credentials: admin@publicos.in/admin@1234, citizen1@test.com/test1234, official.ka@test.com/test1234 — all verified working.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_-prefixed citizens (temp_off_*@example.com, test_citz_*@example.com) and approved issues in Karnataka & Maharashtra via test_publicos_v2.py. Cleaned up the Mumbai/Maharashtra issue is not deleted but flagged with TEST_ prefix in titles where applicable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: v2 regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_publicos_v2.py — run with: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_publicos_v2.py -v. It depends on three pre-seeded users (admin/citizen1/official.ka) created by the main agent. Photo upload uses /api/upload (multipart 'file', bearer token) → returns {file_id, url:'/api/files/{id}'}. Frontend prepends REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL when rendering. Submit wizard has 5 steps (Category → Details → Media → Location → Review); AI re-classify button lives on Details step. Official portal route is /official (requires role=official OR admin). Reset-password page shows reset-invalid testid for both missing-token and bad-token cases. Admin Closure requests tab and Officials tab confirmed wired to /api/admin/issues?status=closure_requested style endpoints + /api/admin/officials. Backend storage uses Emergent object storage initialized via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY; if storage init fails at boot the /api/upload endpoint will 503 — check 'Object storage initialized' log line. AI classify uses gpt-4o-mini via emergentintegrations and correctly mapped 'streetlight not working' to category 'streetlight' on multiple runs.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Prior iteration's only gap (ResetPassword not pre-validating token) is now fixed and verified — bogus token now shows 'Reset link unavailable / Invalid or already-used reset link' with data-testid='reset-invalid' present immediately on mount instead of after submit.
